name: Andy Gibson
description: American jazz trumpeter, arranger, and composer (1913-1961)
abstract: Albert "Andy" Gibson (November 6, 1913 – February 11, 1961) was an American jazz trumpeter, arranger, and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: And so they got me together with a guy by the name of Andy Gibson. And Andy started writing for us... we got a whole bunch of his arrangements in

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript contains no turns from the [INTERVIEWEE] (Cozy Cole). The mention of Andy Gibson occurs exclusively in [INTERVIEWER] turns, where the interviewer states 'Andy Gibson arranged for me' and discusses Gibson's work with Cab Calloway. There is no evidence in the provided text of a personal

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions making a record with Andy Gibson; the interviewee does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Andy Gibson.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: They had a hell of arrangers in them days, like Andy Gibson. Who else? Guitar player from Kansas City. One of the greatest.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: Andy Gibson was a great arranger. I think he‟s from Cleveland, Ohio. I don‟t know what he played except it was trumpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: Who did the arrangements? Andy Gibson, and he tried to copy as close as he could, but most of them were Duke's things.
